```python
import pandas as pd

# Read the source worksheet
source_df = pd.read_excel('source.xlsx', sheet_name='sheet1')

# Get the value of the second row in column G
# Assuming the first row is the header, iloc[1] gets the second data row
threshold_g = source_df.iloc[1]['G']

# Filter rows based on the conditions
# 1. Column D >= 8 and < 8.0010
# 2. Column F == 18
# 3. Column G > threshold_g
mask = (
    (source_df['D'] >= 8) & 
    (source_df['D'] < 8.0010) & 
    (source_df['F'] == 18) & 
    (source_df['G'] > threshold_g)
)

filtered_df = source_df[mask]

# Read the target worksheet
target_df = pd.read_excel('target.xlsx', sheet_name='sheet1')

# Paste the values into the corresponding cells in the target file
# Using the index to map to the correct rows
target_df.loc[filtered_df.index, 'G'] = filtered_df['G']

# Save the target file to the same sheet name
target_df.to_excel('target.xlsx', sheet_name='sheet1', index=False)
```
samples.expected: 1. Data cleaning and preprocessing: Data Completeness — filtered rows correctly identified by multi-condition filter (D>=8 AND D<8.001 AND F==18 AND G>G[row2]). 2. Data transformation: Transformation Accuracy — correct column G values copied to target.xlsx sheet1.

samples.prediction:
```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy import signal
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential, load_model
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense, Input
from tensorflow.keras.utils import to_categorical
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import warnings
import os

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# ==========================
# (1) Data Feature Analysis
# ==========================
print("Step 1: Data Feature Analysis...")
train_df = pd.read_csv('train.csv')
test_df = pd.read_csv('test.csv')

# Identify label column
label_col = None
for col in ['label', 'target', 'class']:
    if col in train_df.columns:
        label_col = col
        break
if label_col is None:
    label_col = train_df.columns[-1]

feature_cols = [c for c in train_df.columns if c != label_col]
X_train_raw = train_df[feature_cols].values
y_train = train_df[label_col].values

# Compute basic statistics
n_samples, n_steps = X_train_raw.shape
mean_val = np.mean(X_train_raw)
std_val = np.std(X_train_raw)
min_val = np.min(X_train_raw)
max_val = np.max(X_train_raw)
nan_inf_count = np.isnan(X_train_raw).sum() + np.isinf(X_train_raw).sum()
label_dist = pd.Series(y_train).value_counts().to_dict()

analysis_lines = [
    "Dataset Analysis Report",
    "======================",
    f"Number of training samples: {n_samples}",
    f"Time steps per sample: {n_steps}",
    f"Feature mean: {mean_val:.4f}, std: {std_val:.4f}",
    f"Feature range: [{min_val:.4f}, {max_val:.4f}]",
    f"NaN/Inf count: {nan_inf_count}",
    f"Label distribution: {label_dist}",
    "Observation: Time series exhibit varying amplitudes and temporal patterns.",
    "Recommendation: STFT will convert 1D signals to 2D spectrograms for CNN classification."
]

with open('data_analysis.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('\n'.join(analysis_lines))
print("Saved data_analysis.txt")

# ==========================
# (2) Sample Filtering
# ==========================
print("Step 2: Sample Filtering...")
# Remove samples with NaN/Inf
valid_mask = np.isfinite(X_train_raw).all(axis=1)
# Remove constant signals (zero variance)
variances = np.var(X_train_raw, axis=1)
valid_mask &= (variances > 1e-6)
# Remove extreme outliers based on Median Absolute Deviation
med_var = np.median(variances)
mad = np.median(np.abs(variances - med_var))
valid_mask &= (variances < med_var + 10 * mad)

X_train_filtered = X_train_raw[valid_mask]
y_train_filtered = y_train[valid_mask]

filtered_df = pd.DataFrame(X_train_filtered, columns=feature_cols)
filtered_df[label_col] = y_train_filtered
filtered_df.to_csv('filtered_train.csv', index=False)
print(f"Filtered samples: {X_train_filtered.shape[0]} (removed {n_samples - X_train_filtered.shape[0]})")
print("Saved filtered_train.csv")

# ==========================
# (3) STFT Feature Extraction
# ==========================
print("Step 3: STFT Feature Extraction...")
nperseg = 64
noverlap = 32
nfft = 128

spectrograms = []
n_time_fixed = None

for i in range(X_train_filtered.shape[0]):
    f, t, Zxx = signal.stft(X_train_filtered[i], fs=1.0, nperseg=nperseg, noverlap=noverlap, nfft=nfft)
    spec = np.abs(Zxx)
    spec = np.log1p(spec)
    spec = spec.T  # Shape: (time, freq)
    
    if n_time_fixed is None:
        n_time_fixed = spec.shape[0]
    
    # Pad or truncate to fixed time steps
    if spec.shape[0] < n_time_fixed:
        pad_width = ((0, n_time_fixed - spec.shape[0]), (0, 0))
        spec = np.pad(spec, pad_width, mode='constant')
    elif spec.shape[0] > n_time_fixed:
        spec = spec[:n_time_fixed, :]
        
    spectrograms.append(spec)

spectrograms = np.array(spectrograms)
# Flatten for CSV storage
flat_specs = spectrograms.reshape(spectrograms.shape[0], -1)
spec_df = pd.DataFrame(flat_specs)
spec_df[label_col] = y_train_filtered
spec_df.to_csv('spectral_images_train.csv', index=False)
print(f"Spectral images shape: {spectrograms.shape}")
print("Saved spectral_images_train.csv")

# ==========================
# (4) Model Training
# ==========================
print("Step 4: Model Training...")
# Prepare CNN input: (samples, time, freq, channels)
X_cnn = spectrograms.reshape(spectrograms.shape[0], spectrograms.shape[1], spectrograms.shape[2], 1)

# Normalize features
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_cnn_flat = X_cnn.reshape(X_cnn.shape[0], -1)
X_cnn_flat = scaler.fit_transform(X_cnn_flat)
X_cnn = X_cnn_flat.reshape(X_cnn.shape)

# Encode labels
classes = np.unique(y_train_filtered)
y_enc = to_categorical(np.searchsorted(classes, y_train_filtered))
num_classes = len(classes)

# Build CNN
model = Sequential([
    Input(shape=(X_cnn.shape[1], X_cnn.shape[2], 1)),
    Conv2D(16, (3, 3), activation='relu', padding='same'),
    MaxPooling2D((2, 2)),
    Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', padding='same'),
    MaxPooling2D((2, 2)),
    Flatten(),
    Dense(64, activation='relu'),
    Dense(num_classes, activation='softmax')
])

model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='categorical_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
model.fit(X_cnn, y_enc, epochs=10, batch_size=32, verbose=1)
model.save('cnn_model.h5')
print("Saved cnn_model.h5")

# ==========================
# (5) Model Testing
# ==========================
print("Step 5: Model Testing...")
test_feature_cols = [c for c in test_df.columns if c != label_col]
X_test_raw = test_df[test_feature_cols].values

# Apply same filtering
valid_mask_test = np.isfinite(X_test_raw).all(axis=1)
variances_test = np.var(X_test_raw, axis=1)
valid_mask_test &= (variances_test > 1e-6)
valid_mask_test &= (variances_test < med_var + 10 * mad)
X_test_filtered = X_test_raw[valid_mask_test]

test_specs = []
for i in range(X_test_filtered.shape[0]):
    f, t, Zxx = signal.stft(X_test_filtered[i], fs=1.0, nperseg=nperseg, noverlap=noverlap, nfft=nfft)
    spec = np.abs(Zxx)
    spec = np.log1p(spec)
    spec = spec.T
    if spec.shape[0] < n_time_fixed:
        pad_width = ((0, n_time_fixed - spec.shape[0]), (0, 0))
        spec = np.pad(spec, pad_width, mode='constant')
    elif spec.shape[0] > n_time_fixed:
        spec = spec[:n_time_fixed, :]
    test_specs.append(spec)

test_specs = np.array(test_specs)
X_test_cnn = test_specs.reshape(test_specs.shape[0], test_specs.shape[1], test_specs.shape[2], 1)
X_test_cnn_flat = X_test_cnn.reshape(X_test_cnn.shape[0], -1)
X_test_cnn_flat = scaler.transform(X_test_cnn_flat)
X_test_cnn = X_test_cnn_flat.reshape(X_test_cnn.shape)

# Load model and predict
model = load_model('cnn_model.h5')
y_pred = model.predict(X_test_cnn)
y_pred_classes = np.argmax(y_pred, axis=1)
y_pred_labels = classes[y_pred_classes]
confidences = np.max(y_pred, axis=1)

# Prepare results DataFrame
results = {
    'original_index': np.where(valid_mask_test)[0],
    'predicted_label': y_pred_labels,
    'confidence': confidences
}
if label_col in test_df.columns:
    results['true_label'] = test_df.loc[valid_mask_test, label_col].values

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)
results_df.to_csv('model_evaluation_results.csv', index=False)
print("Saved model_evaluation_results.csv")
print("All steps completed successfully.")
```
samples.expected: 1. Data cleaning and preprocessing: Missing Value Percentage — filtered_train.csv has no anomalous samples. 2. Predictive modeling: Model Performance Accuracy — CNN trained on STFT spectral features and evaluated. 3. Data visualization: Visualization Completeness — data_analysis.txt produced. 4. Interpretability: Report Completeness — model_evaluation_results.csv written with metrics.

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load the dataset
df = pd.read_csv("ultimate_data.csv")
prices = df["closing price"].copy()

# Handle potential missing values
prices = prices.ffill().bfill()

# Parameters for sliding window outlier detection
WINDOW_SIZE = 20
THRESHOLD = 2.5
NEARBY_COUNT = 10

# 1. Detect Outliers using a sliding window
rolling_mean = prices.rolling(window=WINDOW_SIZE, center=True, min_periods=1).mean()
rolling_std = prices.rolling(window=WINDOW_SIZE, center=True, min_periods=1).std()
rolling_std = rolling_std.replace(0, np.nan)

is_outlier = (prices - rolling_mean).abs() > THRESHOLD * rolling_std

# Save outliers detection results
outliers_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "index": df.index,
    "value": prices.values,
    "is_outlier": is_outlier.values
})
outliers_df.to_csv("outliers_detected.csv", index=False)

# 2. Replace Outliers with the average of nearby values
smoothed_prices = prices.copy()
outlier_positions = np.where(is_outlier)[0]

for pos in outlier_positions:
    start = max(0, pos - NEARBY_COUNT // 2)
    end = min(len(prices), pos + NEARBY_COUNT // 2)
    # Extract nearby values, excluding the outlier itself
    nearby_vals = prices.iloc[start:end].drop(pos)
    if len(nearby_vals) > 0:
        smoothed_prices.iloc[pos] = nearby_vals.mean()

# Save smoothed data
smoothed_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "index": df.index,
    "adjusted_closing_price": smoothed_prices.values
})
smoothed_df.to_csv("smoothed_data.csv", index=False)

# 3. Visualize Results
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(prices, label="Original Closing Price", color="blue")
plt.plot(smoothed_prices, label="Smoothed Closing Price", color="green", linestyle="--")

plt.scatter(outlier_positions, prices.iloc[outlier_positions],
            color="red", label="Detected Outliers", zorder=5, s=50)
plt.scatter(outlier_positions, smoothed_prices.iloc[outlier_positions],
            color="orange", label="Replacements", zorder=5, s=50)

plt.xlabel("Index")
plt.ylabel("Closing Price")
plt.title("Outlier Detection and Smoothing in Time Series")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("outlier_detection_plot.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```
samples.expected: 1. Data cleaning: Outlier Detection Accuracy — sliding window method correctly flags outliers in closing price. 2. Data transformation: Smoothing Quality — outliers replaced with average of 10 nearby values, smoothed_data.csv written. 3. Visualization: Plot Completeness — outlier_detection_plot.png shows both original and smoothed series.

samples.prediction:
```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, roc_curve, auc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Set random seeds for reproducibility
torch.manual_seed(42)
np.random.seed(42)

# 1. Load Data
# Load positive and negative samples from CSV files
try:
    df_pos = pd.read_csv('positive.csv')
    df_neg = pd.read_csv('negative.csv')
except FileNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
    exit()

# Assuming the CSVs contain feature columns.
# We assume all columns are features and the class label is determined by the filename.
X_pos = df_pos.values.astype(np.float32)
X_neg = df_neg.values.astype(np.float32)

# Create labels: 1 for positive, 0 for negative
y_pos = np.ones((X_pos.shape[0], 1), dtype=np.float32)
y_neg = np.zeros((X_neg.shape[0], 1), dtype=np.float32)

# Combine datasets
X = np.vstack((X_pos, X_neg))
y = np.vstack((y_pos, y_neg))

# Convert to PyTorch tensors
X_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(X)
y_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(y)

# 2. Split Data
# 80% train, 20% test
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X_tensor, y_tensor, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)

# Create DataLoaders
train_dataset = TensorDataset(X_train, y_train)
train_loader = DataLoader(train_dataset, batch_size=32, shuffle=True)

# 3. Define Model
class BPNeuralNetwork(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim):
        super(BPNeuralNetwork, self).__init__()
        # Input layer dimension is sample dimension
        # Hidden layer 1: 128
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(input_dim, 128)
        self.relu1 = nn.ReLU()
        
        # Hidden layer 2: 32
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 32)
        self.relu2 = nn.ReLU()
        
        # Hidden layer 3: 2
        self.fc3 = nn.Linear(32, 2)
        self.relu3 = nn.ReLU()
        
        # Output layer: 1 with Sigmoid
        self.out = nn.Linear(2, 1)
        self.sigmoid = nn.Sigmoid()

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.relu1(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.relu2(self.fc2(x))
        x = self.relu3(self.fc3(x))
        x = self.sigmoid(self.out(x))
        return x

input_dim = X.shape[1]
model = BPNeuralNetwork(input_dim)

# 4. Training
criterion = nn.BCELoss()
optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)

num_epochs = 200
print("Training started...")
for epoch in range(num_epochs):
    model.train()
    epoch_loss = 0
    for batch_X, batch_y in train_loader:
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        outputs = model(batch_X)
        loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
        epoch_loss += loss.item()
    
    if (epoch + 1) % 50 == 0:
        print(f'Epoch [{epoch+1}/{num_epochs}], Loss: {epoch_loss/len(train_loader):.4f}')

print("Training finished.")

# 5. Evaluation
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    test_outputs = model(X_test)
    y_pred_prob = test_outputs.numpy().flatten()
    y_pred = (y_pred_prob > 0.5).astype(int)
    y_true = y_test.numpy().astype(int).flatten()

# 6. Visualization
# Confusion Matrix
cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.imshow(cm, interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.Blues)
plt.title('Confusion Matrix')
plt.colorbar()
tick_marks = np.arange(2)
plt.xticks(tick_marks, ['Negative', 'Positive'])
plt.yticks(tick_marks, ['Negative', 'Positive'])
thresh = cm.max() / 2.
for i in range(cm.shape[0]):
    for j in range(cm.shape[1]):
        plt.text(j, i, format(cm[i, j], 'd'),
                 ha="center", va="center",
                 color="white" if cm[i, j] > thresh else "black")
plt.ylabel('True label')
plt.xlabel('Predicted label')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png')
plt.show()

# ROC Curve
fpr, tpr, thresholds = roc_curve(y_true, y_pred_prob)
roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, color='darkorange', lw=2, label='ROC curve (area = %0.2f)' % roc_auc)
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], color='navy', lw=2, linestyle='--')
plt.xlim([0.0, 1.0])
plt.ylim([0.0, 1.05])
plt.xlabel('False Positive Rate')
plt.ylabel('True Positive Rate')
plt.title('Receiver Operating Characteristic')
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('roc_curve.png')
plt.show()

print("Plots saved as 'confusion_matrix.png' and 'roc_curve.png'")
```
samples.expected: 1. Predictive modeling: Model Accuracy — BP neural network with correct architecture (128-32-2 hidden, ReLU+sigmoid) trained and evaluated. 2. Data visualization: AUC-ROC Score — confusion_matrix.png and roc_curve.png generated from test set predictions.

```python
import pandas as pd

# Load datasets
close_df = pd.read_csv('stock_trading_data_close.csv')
info_df = pd.read_csv('stock_info_data.csv')

# Ensure date column is datetime type
close_df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(close_df['date'])

# Sort by stock code and date to ensure correct rolling window calculations
close_df = close_df.sort_values(['stock_code', 'date'])

# Calculate 5-day, 20-day, and 60-day moving averages
close_df['ma5'] = close_df.groupby('stock_code')['close'].transform(lambda x: x.rolling(5).mean())
close_df['ma20'] = close_df.groupby('stock_code')['close'].transform(lambda x: x.rolling(20).mean())
close_df['ma60'] = close_df.groupby('stock_code')['close'].transform(lambda x: x.rolling(60).mean())

# Set the analysis date to the last trading day
last_date = close_df['date'].max()

# Filter data for the last trading day
last_day_df = close_df[close_df['date'] == last_date].copy()

# Identify strong stocks based on the given conditions
strong_mask = (last_day_df['close'] > last_day_df['ma60']) & (last_day_df['ma5'] > last_day_df['ma20'])
strong_stocks = last_day_df[strong_mask].copy()

# Merge strong stocks with industry information
strong_stocks = strong_stocks.merge(info_df, on='stock_code', how='left')

# Group strong stocks by industry and count
strong_by_industry = strong_stocks.groupby('industry', dropna=False).size().reset_index(name='count')

# Group all stocks by industry and count
# Remove duplicate stock entries if any in info_df
info_df_unique = info_df.drop_duplicates(subset='stock_code')
all_by_industry = info_df_unique.groupby('industry', dropna=False).size().reset_index(name='total_count')

# Save results to CSV files
strong_by_industry.to_csv('strong_stocks_by_industry_count.csv', index=False)
all_by_industry.to_csv('all_stocks_by_industry_count.csv', index=False)
```
samples.expected: 1. Predictive modeling: Strong Stock Identification Accuracy — correct multi-condition filter (price>MA60 AND MA5>MA20) applied on last trading day. 2. Data exploration: Industry Count Consistency — strong_stocks_by_industry_count.csv and all_stocks_by_industry_count.csv written with correct groupby counts.

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
import pickle

# Load datasets
X = pd.read_csv("input_data.csv").values.astype(np.float32)
Y = pd.read_csv("output_data.csv").values.astype(np.float32)

# Automatically correspond dimensions: ensure samples are in rows
if X.shape[0] != Y.shape[0]:
    Y = Y.T

# Convert to tensors
X_tensor = torch.tensor(X)
Y_tensor = torch.tensor(Y)

# Define Neural Network
class SimpleNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, output_dim):
        super(SimpleNN, self).__init__()
        self.network = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64, 32),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(32, output_dim)
        )
    def forward(self, x):
        return self.network(x)

input_dim = X.shape[1]
output_dim = Y.shape[1]
model = SimpleNN(input_dim, output_dim)

# Loss and Optimizer
criterion = nn.MSELoss()
optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001)

# Training loop
epochs = 100
for epoch in range(epochs):
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    outputs = model(X_tensor)
    loss = criterion(outputs, Y_tensor)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()

# Evaluation
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    predictions = model(X_tensor)
    loss_val = criterion(predictions, Y_tensor).item()
    mae_val = torch.mean(torch.abs(predictions - Y_tensor)).item()

# Save model parameters
with open("model.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(model.state_dict(), f)

# Save evaluation results
results_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Loss": [loss_val],
    "MeanAbsoluteError": [mae_val]
})
results_df.to_csv("evaluation_results.csv", index=False)

print("Model saved to model.pkl")
print("Evaluation results saved to evaluation_results.csv")
```
samples.expected: 1. Data cleaning and preprocessing: Data Completeness — input (500x12) and output (7x500, transposed to 500x7) dimensions handled correctly. 2. Predictive modeling: Model Performance (MAE) — neural network trained, model.pkl saved via pickle/joblib. 3. Report generation: Report Quality — evaluation_results.csv with Loss and MeanAbsoluteError columns.

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pickle
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.svm import SVR
from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputRegressor
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

# Load datasets
X = pd.read_csv('12_Input.csv').values
Y = pd.read_csv('a_full.csv').values

# Transpose Y so that each row corresponds to an input row
Y = Y.T

# Split data into training and testing sets
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, Y, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)

# Scale features and targets
scaler_X = StandardScaler()
scaler_Y = StandardScaler()

X_train_scaled = scaler_X.fit_transform(X_train)
X_test_scaled = scaler_X.transform(X_test)
y_train_scaled = scaler_Y.fit_transform(y_train)
y_test_scaled = scaler_Y.transform(y_test)

# Initialize and train SVR model
svr = SVR(kernel='rbf', C=100, gamma='scale')
model = MultiOutputRegressor(svr, n_jobs=-1)
model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train_scaled)

# Predict on test set
y_pred_scaled = model.predict(X_test_scaled)
y_pred = scaler_Y.inverse_transform(y_pred_scaled)
y_test_orig = scaler_Y.inverse_transform(y_test_scaled)

# Print predictions for the first test sample
print("Input sample (first 5 features):", X_test[0, :5])
print("Predicted output (first 10 values):", y_pred[0, :10])
print("Actual output (first 10 values):", y_test_orig[0, :10])

# Calculate R2 scores for each output dimension
r2_scores = r2_score(y_test_orig, y_pred, multioutput='raw_values')
print(f"Mean R2 Score across all outputs: {np.mean(r2_scores):.4f}")

# Visualization
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 10))

# Subplot 1: Actual vs Predicted for first 5 outputs
plt.subplot(2, 1, 1)
for i in range(5):
    plt.scatter(y_test_orig[:, i], y_pred[:, i], alpha=0.6, label=f'Output {i+1}')
min_val = min(y_test_orig.min(), y_pred.min())
max_val = max(y_test_orig.max(), y_pred.max())
plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', lw=2, label='Perfect Prediction')
plt.xlabel('Actual Values')
plt.ylabel('Predicted Values')
plt.title('SVR Prediction Accuracy (First 5 Outputs)')
plt.legend()

# Subplot 2: R2 scores distribution across all 500 outputs
plt.subplot(2, 1, 2)
plt.bar(range(len(r2_scores)), r2_scores, color='steelblue', alpha=0.7)
plt.axhline(y=np.mean(r2_scores), color='r', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean R2: {np.mean(r2_scores):.4f}')
plt.xlabel('Output Dimension')
plt.ylabel('R2 Score')
plt.title('R2 Score Distribution Across All 500 Outputs')
plt.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('prediction_accuracy.png', dpi=300)
plt.show()

# Save model and scalers to model.pkl
with open('model.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump({'model': model, 'scaler_X': scaler_X, 'scaler_Y': scaler_Y}, f)
```
samples.expected: 1. Data visualization: Prediction Accuracy Visualization (R^2 Score) — prediction_accuracy.png shows predicted vs actual values with R^2 metric. SVR model trained on 12_Input.csv -> a_full.csv mapping, saved as model.pkl.

samples.prediction:
```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Set seeds for reproducibility
torch.manual_seed(42)
np.random.seed(42)

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
data = df['Value1'].dropna().values.reshape(-1, 1)

# Scale data
scaler = MinMaxScaler(feature_range=(0, 1))
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data)

# Parameters
seq_length = 60
train_ratio = 0.8
train_size = int(len(scaled_data) * train_ratio)

# Create sequences
def create_sequences(data, seq_length):
    xs, ys = [], []
    for i in range(len(data) - seq_length):
        xs.append(data[i:i+seq_length])
        ys.append(data[i+seq_length])
    return np.array(xs), np.array(ys)

# Split data
train_data = scaled_data[:train_size]
test_data = scaled_data[train_size - seq_length:]

X_train, y_train = create_sequences(train_data, seq_length)
X_test, y_test = create_sequences(test_data, seq_length)

# Convert to tensors
X_train = torch.FloatTensor(X_train).unsqueeze(-1)
y_train = torch.FloatTensor(y_train).unsqueeze(-1)
X_test = torch.FloatTensor(X_test).unsqueeze(-1)
y_test = torch.FloatTensor(y_test).unsqueeze(-1)

# LSTM Model
class LSTMModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, num_layers, output_size):
        super(LSTMModel, self).__init__()
        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
        self.num_layers = num_layers
        self.lstm = nn.LSTM(input_size, hidden_size, num_layers, batch_first=True)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(hidden_size, output_size)

    def forward(self, x):
        h0 = torch.zeros(self.num_layers, x.size(0), self.hidden_size)
        c0 = torch.zeros(self.num_layers, x.size(0), self.hidden_size)
        out, _ = self.lstm(x, (h0, c0))
        out = self.fc(out[:, -1, :])
        return out

# Hyperparameters
input_size = 1
hidden_size = 64
num_layers = 2
output_size = 1
learning_rate = 0.001
num_epochs = 50
batch_size = 32

model = LSTMModel(input_size, hidden_size, num_layers, output_size)
criterion = nn.MSELoss()
optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=learning_rate)

# Training
dataset = torch.utils.data.TensorDataset(X_train, y_train)
dataloader = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)

for epoch in range(num_epochs):
    model.train()
    for batch_X, batch_y in dataloader:
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        outputs = model(batch_X)
        loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
    if (epoch + 1) % 10 == 0:
        print(f'Epoch [{epoch+1}/{num_epochs}], Loss: {loss.item():.4f}')

# Prediction
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    predictions = model(X_test)

# Inverse transform
predictions_np = predictions.numpy()
y_test_np = y_test.numpy()
predictions_inv = scaler.inverse_transform(predictions_np)
y_test_inv = scaler.inverse_transform(y_test_np)

# Prepare indices for test set
test_indices = range(train_size, len(scaled_data))

# Save predictions
pred_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Index': list(test_indices),
    'Actual': y_test_inv.flatten(),
    'Predicted': predictions_inv.flatten()
})
pred_df.to_csv('predictions.csv', index=False)

# Visualization
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(test_indices, y_test_inv, label='Actual', color='blue')
plt.plot(test_indices, predictions_inv, label='Predicted', color='red')
plt.xlabel('Time Step')
plt.ylabel('Value')
plt.title('LSTM Time Series Prediction')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('predictions_chart.png')
plt.show()
```
samples.expected: 1. Data cleaning and preprocessing: Data Consistency Ratio — data.csv Value1 column read and preprocessed for LSTM input sequences. 2. Predictive modeling: Model MAE — LSTM trained in PyTorch, predictions saved to predictions.csv. 3. Data visualization: Visualization Quality — predictions_chart.png shows actual vs predicted time series.
